deployHtml:
apiVersion: k0rdent.mirantis.com/v1beta1
kind: MultiClusterService
metadata:
  name: opensearch
spec:
  clusterSelector:
    matchLabels:
      group: demo
  serviceSpec:
    services:
    - template: traefik-39-0-8
      name: traefik
      namespace: traefik
      values: |
        traefik:
          deployment:
            kind: DaemonSet
          ports:
            web:
              port: 8000
              hostPort: 80
            websecure:
              port: 8443
              hostPort: 443
    - template: raw-2-0-2
      name: raw
      namespace: open-webui
      values: |
        raw:
          resources:
          - apiVersion: v1
            kind: Secret
            metadata:
              name: opensearch-auth
              namespace: open-webui
            type: Opaque
            stringData:
              password: InitPass.123
    - template: opensearch-3-3-2
      name: opensearch
      namespace: open-webui
      dependsOn:
      - name: raw
        namespace: open-webui
      values: |
        opensearch:
          singleNode: true
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: 1Gi
              cpu: 500m
          extraEnvs:
            - name: OPENSEARCH_INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: opensearch-auth
                  key: password
    - template: tika-3-2-2
      name: tika
      namespace: tika
    - template: open-webui-14-1-0
      name: open-webui
      namespace: open-webui
      dependsOn:
      - name: raw
        namespace: open-webui
      values: |
        open-webui:
          ollama:
            ollama:
              models:
                pull: [gemma3:1b]
                run: [gemma3:1b]
          ingress:
            enabled: true
            host: ''
    
          extraEnvVars:
            - name: VECTOR_DB
              value: opensearch
            - name: OPENSEARCH_URI
              value: https://opensearch-cluster-master.open-webui:9200
            - name: OPENSEARCH_USERNAME
              value: admin
            - name: OPENSEARCH_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: opensearch-auth
                  key: password
            - name: OPENSEARCH_SSL
              value: "True"
            - name: OPENSEARCH_CERT_VERIFY
              value: "False"
            - name: CONTENT_EXTRACTION_ENGINE
              value: tika
            - name: TIKA_SERVER_URL
              value: http://tika.tika:9998
            - name: ENABLE_PERSISTENT_CONFIG
              value: "False"

contentHtml:
RAG and Vector Search Stack

A curated stack for building Retrieval-Augmented Generation applications. Combines full-text and vector search with document processing and a self-hosted AI chat interface for enterprise knowledge bases and semantic search systems.

The k0rdent Catalog simplifies deploying and managing production-ready platform services across multiple Kubernetes clusters. Catalog applications are packaged as service templates that can be installed once and then deployed consistently across many clusters using MultiClusterService resources.

This solution deploys a RAG-ready stack consisting of:

  • Traefik – provides a standard Kubernetes ingress controller for exposing the chat UI
  • OpenSearch – search and analytics engine with vector search capabilities for RAG document retrieval
  • Tika – extracts text from PDFs, Office documents, and 1000+ formats for ingestion into the search index
  • Open WebUI – self-hosted AI chat interface with built-in RAG support, document upload, and retrieval

Together, these components provide a production RAG pipeline:

  • Users upload documents through Open WebUI, which are processed by Tika for text extraction
  • OpenSearch indexes documents and provides full-text and vector similarity search
  • Open WebUI retrieves relevant context from OpenSearch to augment LLM responses
  • Traefik exposes the Open WebUI interface for external access

Using the k0rdent Catalog service templates, this stack can be installed once and then deployed automatically to any number of clusters.

Prerequisites

Deploy k0rdent v1.0.0: QuickStart

Install template to k0rdent

helm upgrade --install traefik oci://ghcr.io/k0rdent/catalog/charts/kgst \
  --set "chart=traefik:39.0.8" \
  -n kcm-system
helm upgrade --install raw oci://ghcr.io/k0rdent/catalog/charts/kgst \
  --set "chart=raw:2.0.2" \
  -n kcm-system
helm upgrade --install opensearch oci://ghcr.io/k0rdent/catalog/charts/kgst \
  --set "chart=opensearch:3.3.2" \
  -n kcm-system
helm upgrade --install tika oci://ghcr.io/k0rdent/catalog/charts/kgst \
  --set "chart=tika:3.2.2" \
  -n kcm-system
helm upgrade --install open-webui oci://ghcr.io/k0rdent/catalog/charts/kgst \
  --set "chart=open-webui:14.1.0" \
  -n kcm-system

Verify service template

kubectl get servicetemplates -A
# NAMESPACE    NAME                            VALID
# kcm-system   traefik-39-0-8                  true
# kcm-system   raw-2-0-2                       true
# kcm-system   opensearch-3-3-2                true
# kcm-system   tika-3-2-2                      true
# kcm-system   open-webui-14-1-0               true

Deploy stack

apiVersion: k0rdent.mirantis.com/v1beta1
kind: MultiClusterService
metadata:
  name: opensearch
spec:
  clusterSelector:
    matchLabels:
      group: demo
  serviceSpec:
    services:
    - template: traefik-39-0-8
      name: traefik
      namespace: traefik
      values: |
        traefik:
          deployment:
            kind: DaemonSet
          ports:
            web:
              port: 8000
              hostPort: 80
            websecure:
              port: 8443
              hostPort: 443
    - template: raw-2-0-2
      name: raw
      namespace: open-webui
      values: |
        raw:
          resources:
          - apiVersion: v1
            kind: Secret
            metadata:
              name: opensearch-auth
              namespace: open-webui
            type: Opaque
            stringData:
              password: InitPass.123
    - template: opensearch-3-3-2
      name: opensearch
      namespace: open-webui
      dependsOn:
      - name: raw
        namespace: open-webui
      values: |
        opensearch:
          singleNode: true
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: 1Gi
              cpu: 500m
          extraEnvs:
            - name: OPENSEARCH_INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: opensearch-auth
                  key: password
    - template: tika-3-2-2
      name: tika
      namespace: tika
    - template: open-webui-14-1-0
      name: open-webui
      namespace: open-webui
      dependsOn:
      - name: raw
        namespace: open-webui
      values: |
        open-webui:
          ollama:
            ollama:
              models:
                pull: [gemma3:1b]
                run: [gemma3:1b]
          ingress:
            enabled: true
            host: ''
    
          extraEnvVars:
            - name: VECTOR_DB
              value: opensearch
            - name: OPENSEARCH_URI
              value: https://opensearch-cluster-master.open-webui:9200
            - name: OPENSEARCH_USERNAME
              value: admin
            - name: OPENSEARCH_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: opensearch-auth
                  key: password
            - name: OPENSEARCH_SSL
              value: "True"
            - name: OPENSEARCH_CERT_VERIFY
              value: "False"
            - name: CONTENT_EXTRACTION_ENGINE
              value: tika
            - name: TIKA_SERVER_URL
              value: http://tika.tika:9998
            - name: ENABLE_PERSISTENT_CONFIG
              value: "False"

Testing in child cluster

1. Access Open WebUI at the configured ingress host.
  1. Upload a document (PDF, DOCX, etc.) through the Open WebUI interface to test the RAG pipeline.

3. Ask a question about the uploaded document content to verify retrieval-augmented generation is working. Verify the document is stored to OpenSearch: 

kubectl run debug \
  -n open-webui \
  --rm -it \
  --restart=Never \
  --image=curlimages/curl \
  -- curl -k -u admin:InitPass.123 \
  'https://opensearch-cluster-master.open-webui:9200/_cat/indices?v'
Expected output: 
# health status index                                                uuid                   pri rep docs.count docs.deleted store.size pri.store.size
# yellow open   open_webui_file-31747fde-8f3c-4a8b-86ae-12f3887e217e rYU5zjNoQriWKdhsS1-6Jg   1   1         59            0    245.8kb        245.8kb
# green  open   .plugins-ml-config                                   O5yoyJvCRHaiNqtiyr3-DA   1   0          1            0        4kb            4kb
# green  open   top_queries-2026.06.15-14720                         LMy4K4U8TquxgRu9uJ76XQ   1   0          8            0     69.3kb         69.3kb
# green  open   .opendistro_security                                 YeO2BqbaQzymdPZN-fqQ7A   1   0          9            0     80.8kb         80.8kb
# green  open   security-auditlog-2026.06.15                         EWAm4kiqRVm6IdBzkHF7nw   1   0          7            0    106.8kb        106.8kb
... there is open_webui_file-... item stored related to the uploaded file.

4. Verify OpenSearch is running: 

kubectl get pods -n open-webui

5. Verify Tika is running: 

kubectl get pods -n tika
